clinical_summary: Patient demonstrates sepsis risk criteria with elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L), leukocytosis (14.2 K/uL), and anemia (Hgb 9.8 g/dL). Progressive AKI is evident with stage 1 (Cr 2.1 mg/dL) progressing to stage 3 (Cr 8.9 mg/dL). Thrombocytopenia (88-95 K/uL) meets SOFA coagulopathy criteria. Bilirubin elevation (1.4 mg/dL) suggests possible hepatic involvement.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and AKI stage 3 meet Sepsis-3 criteria (suspected infection + SOFA increase ≥2)
prioritized_risks.threshold: Suspected infection plus acute SOFA increase ≥2

prioritized_risks.explanation: Creatinine rose 6.5 mg/dL in 16 hours (excluded outlier) with prior stage 1 elevation
prioritized_risks.threshold: Creatinine rise ≥0.3 mg/dL in 48h or ≥1.5x baseline

prioritized_risks.explanation: Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA score 3 criteria with high bleeding risk
prioritized_risks.threshold: Platelets <150 K/uL initiates SOFA coagulation scoring

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L present but hemodynamic data unavailable to confirm vasopressor dependence

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per local sepsis protocol
Order lactate clearance measurement and repeat in 2 hours
Start fluid resuscitation and assess need for vasopressors
Obtain blood cultures and infectious workup
Monitor platelet trends and consider transfusion if <50 K/uL
doctor_handoff: Review source control possibilities (imaging pending), assess for response to initial sepsis bundle, and evaluate for renal replacement therapy eligibility.

disease_progression.observation: Initial sepsis markers: WBC 14.2, Hgb 9.8, Bilirubin 1.4

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
